name: bento-meta

metadata.name: bento-meta
metadata.summary: Python drivers for Bento Metamodel Database

documentation:
# bento_meta - Object Model for the Bento Metamodel Database

Read the docs at
[https://cbiit.github.io/bento-meta/](https://cbiit.github.io/bento-meta/).

modules.name: bento_meta.mdb.searchable
documentation:
mdb.searchable
Subclass of `class:bento_meta.mdb.MDB` to support searching fulltext indices on an MDB
Note: certain fulltext indexes on certain MDB nodes and properties must be present in 
the Neo4j instance: 
- entityHandles
- termValue
- termDefn
- termValueDefn

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.searchable.SearchableMDB.available_indexes
bento_meta.mdb.searchable.SearchableMDB.query_index
documentation:
Query a named fulltext index of nodes or relationships.
Returns [ {ent:{}, label:<label>, score:<lucene score>} ].

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.searchable.SearchableMDB.search_entity_handles
bento_meta.mdb.searchable.SearchableMDB.search_terms
documentation:
Fulltext for qstring over terms, by value, definition, or both (default).
Returns [ { ent:<term dict>, score:<lucene score> } ]}

modules.name: bento_meta.mdb
documentation:
# bento_meta.mdb

This module contains {class}`MDB`, with machinery for efficiently
querying a Neo4j instance of a Metamodel Database.

modules.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools
documentation: Imports functions from mdb_tools

modules.name: bento_meta.mdb.loaders
documentation: mdb.loaders: load models into an MDB instance consistently

functions.name: bento_meta.mdb.loaders.load_mdf
documentation: Load an MDF object into an MDB instance

functions.name: bento_meta.mdb.loaders.load_model
documentation: Load a model object into an MDB instance.

functions.name: bento_meta.mdb.loaders.load_model_statements
documentation:
Create Cypher statements from a model to load it de novo into an
MDB instance.

\:param {class}`mdb.Model` model: Model instance for loading
\:param str \_commit: 'Commit string' for marking entities in DB. If set, this will override
\_commit attributes already existing on Model entities.

modules.name: bento_meta.mdb.writeable
documentation: mdb.writeable: subclass of `class:bento_meta.MDB` to support writing to an MDB

functions.name: bento_meta.mdb.writeable.write_txn
documentation:
Decorates a query function to run a write transaction based
on its query.
Query function should return a tuple (qry_string, param_dict).

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.writeable.WriteableMDB.put_with_statement
documentation: Run an arbitrary write statement.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.writeable.WriteableMDB.put_term_with_origin
documentation:
Merge a bento-meta Term object, that has an Origin object set,
into an MDB. If a new term is created, assign a random 6-char nanoid 
to it. The Origin must already be represented in the database.
:param Term term: Term object
:param str commit: GitHub commit SHA1 associated with the term (if any)

modules.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb
documentation:
# bento_meta.mdb

This module contains {class}`MDB`, with machinery for efficiently
querying a Neo4j instance of a Metamodel Database.

The constructor queries the database for registered models.
The attribute models : Dict contains model handles (names) as keys,
and a list of version strings as values.
The attribute latest_version : Dict contains model handles as keys
and the version string tagged "is_latest" as values.

functions.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.read_txn
documentation:
Decorates a query function to run a read transaction based on
its query.
Query function should return a tuple (qry_string, param_dict).
Returns list of driver Records.
Decorator functions to produce executed transactions based on an
underlying query/param function:

functions.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.read_txn_value
documentation:
Decorates a query function to run a read transaction based on
its query.
Query function should return a tuple (qry_string, param_dict, values_key).
Returns list of values for key specified by query function.

functions.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.read_txn_data
documentation:
Decorates a query function to run a read transaction based on
its query.
Query function should return a tuple (qry_string, param_dict).
Returns records as a list of simple dicts.

functions.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.make_nanoid
documentation: Create a random nanoid and return it as a string.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.__init__
documentation:
Create an {class}`MDB` object, with a connection to a Neo4j instance of a metamodel database.
\:param bolt_url uri: The Bolt protocol endpoint to the Neo4j instance (default, use the
`NEO4J_MDB_URI` env variable)
\:param str user: Username for Neo4j access (default, use the `NEO4J_MDB_USER` env variable)
\:param str password: Password for user (default, use the `NEO4J_MDB_PASS` env variable)

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.register_txfn
documentation:
Register a transaction function
(see https://neo4j.com/docs/api/python-driver/current/api.html#managed-transactions-transaction-functions)
with the class for later use.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_model_info
documentation: Get models, versions, and latest versions from MDB Model nodes
def run_txfn(self, name, *args, **kwargs):

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_model_handles
documentation:
Return a simple list of model handles available.
Queries Model nodes (not model properties in Entity nodes)

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_model_versions
documentation:
Get list of version strings present in database for a given model.
Returns [ <string> ].

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_latest_version
documentation:
Get the version string from Model node marked is_latest:True for a given
model handle.
Returns <string>

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_model_nodes
documentation:
Return a list of dicts representing Model nodes.
Returns all versions.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_nodes_by_model
documentation:
Get all nodes for a given model.
If :param:model is set but :param:version is None, get nodes from model version
marked is_latest:true
If :param:model is set and :param:version is '*', get nodes from all model versions.
If :param:model is None, get all nodes in database.
Returns [ <node> ].

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_model_nodes_edges
documentation:
Get all node-relationship-node paths for a given model and version.
If :param:version is None, use version marked is_latest:true for
:param:model.
If :param:version is '*', retrieve from all versions.
Returns [ path ]

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_node_edges_by_node_id
documentation:
Get incoming and outgoing relationship information for a node,
given its nanoid.
Returns [ {id, handle, model, version, near_type, far_type, rln, far_node} ].

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_node_and_props_by_node_id
documentation:
Get a node and its properties, given the node nanoid.
Returns [ {id, handle, model, version, node, props[]} ].

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_nodes_and_props_by_model
documentation:
Get all nodes with associated properties given a model handle.
If model is None, get all nodes with their properties.
If :param:model is set but :param:version is None, get nodes and props
from model version marked is_latest:true
If :param:model is set and :param:version is '*', get nodes and props
from all model versions.

Returns [ {id, handle, model, version, props[]} ]

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_prop_node_and_domain_by_prop_id
documentation:
Get a property, its node, and its value domain or value set
of terms, given the property nanoid.
Returns [ { id, handle, model, version, value_domain, prop, node, value_set, terms[] } ].

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_valueset_by_id
documentation:
Get a valueset with the properties that use it and the terms
that constitute it.
Returns [ {id, handle, url, terms[], props[]} ]

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_valuesets_by_model
documentation:
Get all valuesets that are used by properties in the given
model and version (or all valuesets if model is None).
Also return list of properties using each valueset.
If version is None, get value sets associated with latest model version.
If version is '*', get those associated with all versions of given model.
Returns [ {value_set, props[]} ].

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_term_by_id
documentation:
Get a term having the given nanoid, with its origin.
Returns {term, origin}.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_props_and_terms_by_model
documentation:
Get terms from valuesets associated with properties in a given model
and version (or all such terms if model is None).
If version is None, get props and terms from the latest model version.
If version is set to '*', get those from all versions of the given model.
Returns [ {prop, terms[]} ]

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_origins
documentation:
Get all origins.
Returns [ <origin> ]

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_origin_by_id
documentation: Get an origin by nanoid.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_tags_for_entity_by_id
documentation:
Get all tags attached to an entity, given the entity's nanoid.
Returns [ {model(str), tags[]} ].

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_tags_and_values
documentation:
Get all tag key/value pairs that are present in database.
Returns [ { key(str) : values[] } ]

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_entities_by_tag
documentation:
Get all entities, tagged with a given key or key:value pair.
Returns [ {tag_key(str), tag_value(str), entity(str - label), entities[]} ]

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb.MDB.get_with_statement
documentation: Run an arbitrary read statement and return data.

modules.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools
documentation:
ToolsMDB: subclass of 'WriteableMDB' to support interactions with the MDB.

EntityValidator: validates that entities have required attributes.

functions.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools._get_nlp_model
documentation: Installs and imports spacy & scispacy nlp model if any not already installed

classes.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB._get_entity_count
documentation:
Returns count of given entity found in an MDB.

If count = 0, entity with given attributes not found in MDB.
If count = 1, entity with given attributes is unique in MDB
If count > 1, more attributes needed to uniquely id entity in MDB.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB._get_pattern_count
documentation:
Returns count of given match pattern, which could be a triple like:
(n)-[r]->(m), or a set of overlapping triples (Path) found in MDB.

If count = 0, pattern with given attributes not found in MDB.
If count = 1, pattern with given attributes is unique in MDB
If count > 1, more attributes needed to uniquely id pattern in MDB.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.validate_entity_unique
documentation:
Validates that the given entity occurs once (& only once) in an MDB

Raises EntityNotUniqueError if entity attributes match multiple property
    graph nodes in the MDB.

Raises EntityNotFoundError if entity attributes don't match any in the MDB.

Note: doesn't validate the entity itself because not all entity attibutes
    necessarily required to locate an entity in the MDB.
    (e.g. handle and model OR nanoid alone can identify a node)

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.validate_entities_unique
documentation: Runs self.validate_entity_unique() over multiple entities

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.validate_pattern_unique
documentation:
Validates that the given match pattern occurs once (& only once) in an MDB

Raises PatternNotUniqueError if entity attributes match multiple property
graph nodes in the MDB.

Raises PatternNotFoundError if entity attributes don't match any in the MDB.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.remove_entity_from_mdb
documentation:
Remove given Entity node from the database.

Accepts the following bento-meta Entities:
    Concept, Node, Predicate, Property, Edge, Term

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.add_entity_to_mdb
documentation: Adds given Entity node to MDB instance

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.get_concept_nanoids_linked_to_entity
documentation:
Returns list of concept nanoids linked to given entity by
"represents" or "has_concept" relationships tagged with
the given mapping source.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.add_relationship_to_mdb
documentation: Adds relationship between given entities in MDB.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.link_synonyms
documentation:
Link two synonymous entities in the MDB via a Concept node.

This function takes two synonymous Entities (as determined by user/SME) as
bento-meta objects connects them to a Concept node via a 'represents' relationship.

Entities must both exist in the MDB instance and given entity attributes must
uniquely identify property graph nodes in the MDB.

If _commit is set (to a string), the _commit property of any node created is set to
this value.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.get_entity_nanoid
documentation: Takes a unique entity in the MDB and returns its nanoid.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.get_or_make_entity_nanoid
documentation: Obtains existing entity's nanoid or creates one for new entity.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.get_term_nanoids
documentation: Returns list of term nanoids representing given concept

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.get_predicate_nanoids
documentation: Returns list of predicate nanoids with relationship to given concept

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.get_relationship_between_entities
documentation: Returns relationship type between given entities with (src)-[:rel_type]->(dst)

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.link_concepts_via_predicate
documentation:
Links two synonymous Concepts via a Predicate

This function takes two synonymous Concepts as objects and links
them via a Predicate node and has_subject and has_object relationships.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.merge_two_concepts
documentation:
Combine two synonymous Concepts into a single Concept.

This function takes two synonymous Concept as bento-meta objects and
merges them into a single Concept along with any connected Terms and Predicates.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB._get_all_terms
documentation: Returns list of all terms in an MDB.

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.get_potential_term_synonyms
documentation:
Returns list of dicts representing potential Term nodes synonymous to given Term

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.potential_synonyms_to_csv
documentation: Given a list of synonymous Terms as dicts, outputs to CSV file at given output path

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.link_term_synonyms_csv
documentation: Given a CSV of syonymous Terms, links each via a Concept node to given Term

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.get_property_synonyms_direct
documentation: Returns list of properties linked by concept to given property

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB._get_property_synonyms_direct_as_list
documentation:
converts results of read_txn_data-wrapped function
with one item to a simple list of bento_meta.objects.Property entities

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.get_property_synonyms_all
documentation:
Returns list of properties linked by concept to given property
or to synonym of given property (and so on)

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB._get_property_parents_data
documentation:
get list of nodes/edges connected to given property
via the "has_property" relationship

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.get_property_parents
documentation:
returns results of _get_property_parents_data as a list of
bento_meta Nodes or Edges

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.add_tag_to_mdb_entity
documentation: Adds a tag to an existing entity in an MDB.

inner_classes.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.ToolsMDB.EntityNotFoundError

Raised when a match pattern's attributes identify more than 1

documentation:
Raised when a match pattern's attributes fail to identify
a property graph triple or set of overlapping triples in an MDB
Adds mdb-tools to WriteableMDB

classes.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.EntityValidator

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.EntityValidator.validate_entity_has_attribute
documentation: Validates the presence of an entity's attribute

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.EntityValidator._validate_entity_attribute
documentation: Checks that an entity attribute is in a set of valid attributes

methods.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.EntityValidator.validate_entity
documentation:
Checks if entity has all attributes required by MDB for its type, and
that all of those attributes are valid themselves if they are entities or
if they have a fixed set of possible values.

Verifies that bento-meta entity has all necesssary attributes before
it is added to an MDB instance.

If looking for a unique identifier for the entity (i.e. nanoid), ensures
entity has all the required attributes for unique identification.

inner_classes.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.EntityValidator.MissingAttributeError
documentation: Raised when an entity doesn't have the attributes required for unique identification

inner_classes.name: bento_meta.mdb.mdb_tools.mdb_tools.EntityValidator.InvalidAttributeError
documentation: Raised when an entity attribute is invalid
Entity validator that validate entities have all required attributes

modules.name: bento_meta.objects
documentation:
# bento_meta.objects

This module contains the subclasses of {class}`Entity` which are used
in representing the models contained in the [MDB](https://github.com/CBIIT/bento-mdf).

functions.name: bento_meta.objects.mergespec
documentation:
Merge subclass attribute and mapping specification dicts with the
base class's. Not for human consumption.

A 3-tuple that fully qualifies the edge: `(edge.handle, src.handle, dst.handle)`

classes.name: bento_meta.objects.ValueSet

methods.name: bento_meta.objects.ValueSet.__setattr__
documentation: @property
def dirty(self):
return self.pvt.dirty
@dirty.setter
def dirty(self, value):
print("Dirty, dirty value set!")
self.pvt.dirty = value
if self.prop and value != 0:
self.prop.dirty=1

Subclass that models an enumerated set of {class}`Property` values.
Essentially a container for {class}`Term` instances.
for ValueSet - updating terms prop should dirty the connected Property
(from Bento::Meta), signal need to refresh. Engineer so this happens
here (__setattr__ override), not in Entity

modules.name: bento_meta.object_map
documentation:
# bento_meta.object_map

This module contains {class}`ObjectMap`, a class which provides the
machinery for mapping bento_meta objects to a Bento Metamodel Database
in Neo4j. Mostly not for human consumption. The ObjectMap:

- interprets the attribute specification (attspec) and map
  specification (mapspec) associated with {class}`Entity` subclasses
- provides the {meth}`get` and {meth}`put` methods to subclasses, that
  enable them to get and put themselves to the database
- generates appropriate [Cypher](https://neo4j.com/docs/cypher-manual/current/) queries to do gets and puts

One ObjectMap instance should be generated for each Entity subclass (see, e.g.,
{class}`bento_meta.model.Model`)

classes.name: bento_meta.object_map.ObjectMap

methods.name: bento_meta.object_map.ObjectMap.get_by_id
documentation: Get an entity given an id attribute value (not the Neo4j id)

documentation:
PROTOTYPE
Get an entity given an id attribute value (not the Neo4j id)

methods.name: bento_meta.object_map.ObjectMap.get
documentation: Get the data for an object instance from the db and load the instance with it

methods.name: bento_meta.object_map.ObjectMap.put
documentation: Put the object instance's attributes to the mapped data node in the database

methods.name: bento_meta.object_map.ObjectMap.rm
documentation: 'Delete' the object's mapped node from the database

methods.name: bento_meta.object_map.ObjectMap.add
documentation:
Create a link between an object instance and a target object in the database.
This represents adding an object-valued attribute to the object.

methods.name: bento_meta.object_map.ObjectMap.drop
documentation:
Remove an existing link between an object instance and a target object in the database.
This represents dropping an object-valued attribute from the object.

methods.name: bento_meta.object_map.ObjectMap.get_owners
documentation: Get the nodes which are linked to the object instance (the owners of the object)

documentation: PROTOTYPE

This module contains {class}`ObjectMap`, a class which provides the

in Neo4j. Mostly not for human consumption.

modules.name: bento_meta.entity
documentation:
bento_meta.entity
=================

This module contains
* `Entity`, the base class for metamodel objects,
* the `CollValue` class to manage collection-valued attributes, and
* the `ArgError` exception.

classes.name: bento_meta.entity.ArgError
documentation: Exception for method argument errors.

classes.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.__init__
documentation:
Entity constructor. Always called by subclasses.

```
.. py:function:: Node(init)
```

```
param dict init

A dict of attribute names and values. Undeclared attributes are ignored.

param neo4j.graph.Node init

a  object to be stored as a model object.

param  init

an Entity (of matching subclass). Used to duplicate another model object.
```

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.mapspec
documentation: The object to database mapping specification. Is a class method, not a property.

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.versioning
documentation:
Get or set whether versioning is applied to object manipulations.

    :param boolean on: True, apply versioning. False, do not.

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.set_version_count
documentation:
Set the integer version counter.

This will usually be accessed via a `bento_meta.Model` instance.

    :param int ct: Set version counter to this value

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.default
documentation: Returns a default value for the property named, or None if no default defined.

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.get_by_id
documentation:
Get an object from the db with the id attribute (not the Neo4j id).

Returns a new object.
:param string id: value of id for desired object
@classmethod

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.dirty
documentation:
Flag whether this instance has been changed since retrieval from the database.

Set to -1, ensure that the next time an attribute is accessed, the instance
will retrieve itself from the database.

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.versioned
documentation: Is this instance versioned?

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.belongs
documentation:
Dict that stores information on the owners (referents) of this instance
in the model

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.dup
documentation:
Duplicate the object, but not too deeply.

Mainly for use of the versioning machinery.

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.delete
documentation:
Delete self from the database.

If versioning is active, this will 'deprecate' the entity, but not actually remove it from the db

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.dget
bento_meta.entity.Entity.dput
documentation:
Put self to the database.

This will set the `neoid` property if not yet set.

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.rm
documentation: Delete self from the database. The object instance survives.

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.attr_doc
documentation: Create a docstring for declared attributes on class as configured.

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.get_label
documentation: Return type of entity as label.

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.Entity.get_attr_dict
documentation:
Return simple attributes set for Entity as a dict.

Attr values are converted to strings. Doesn't include attrs with None values.

Base class for all metamodel objects.

Entity contains all the magic for metamodel objects such as
`bento_meta.objects.Node` and 'bento_meta.object.Edge`. It will rarely
be used directly. Entity redefines `__setattr__` and `__getattr__` to
enable necessary bookkeeping for model versioning and graph database
object mapping under the hood.

The Entity class also defines private and declared attributes that are
common to all metamodel objects. It provides the machinery to manage
private attributes separately from declared attributes, and to raise
exceptions when attempts are made to access attributes that are not
declared.

classes.name: bento_meta.entity.CollValue

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.CollValue.owner
documentation: The entity instance of which this collection is an attribute.

methods.name: bento_meta.entity.CollValue.owner_key
documentation: The attribute name of this collection on the `owner`.

A UserDict for housing Entity collection attributes.

This class contains a hook for recording the Entity that
owns the value that is being set. The value is marked as belonging
to the *containing object*, not this collection object.
It also protects against adding arbitrarily typed elements to the
collection; it throws unless a value to set is an `Entity`. `__setitem__`
is instrumented for managing versioning.

```
param owner

 object of which this collection is an attribute

param owner_key

the attribute name of this collection on the owner
```

modules.name: bento_meta.model
documentation:
# bento_meta.model

This module contains {class}`Model`, a class for managing data models housed
in the Bento Metamodel Database. Models are built from `bento_meta.Entity`
subclasses (see {mod}`bento_meta.objects`). A Model can be used with or
without a Neo4j database connection.

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.__init__
documentation:
Model constructor.

```
param str handle

A string name for the model. Corresponds to the model property in MDB database nodes.

param bento_meta.mdb.MDB mdb

An MDB object containing the db connection (see )
```

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.versioning
documentation:
Get or set versioning state.

```
param boolean on

True, apply versioning. False, do not.
```

Note: this delegates to {meth}`Entity.versioning`.

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.set_version_count
documentation:
Set the integer version counter.

```
param int ct

Set version counter to this value.
```

Note: this delegates to {meth}`Entity.set_version_count`.

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.drv
documentation: Neo4j database driver from MDB object

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.add_node
documentation:
Add a {class}`Node` to the model.

```
param Node node

A  instance, a , or a dict
```

The model attribute of `node` is set to `Model.handle`

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.add_edge
documentation:
Add an {class}`Edge` to the model.

```
param Edge edge

A  instance, a , or a dict
```

The model attribute of `edge` is set to `Model.handle`

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.add_prop
documentation:
Add a {class}`Property` to the model.

```
param Node|Edge ent

Attach prop to this entity

param Property prop

A  instance, a :class: , or a dict

param boolean reuse

If True, reuse existing property with same handle
```

The model attribute of `prop` is set to `Model.handle`. Within a model,
{class}`Property` entities are unique with respect to their
handle (but can be reused). This method will look for an existing
property within the model with the given handle, and add an item to
Model.props pointing to it if found.

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.annotate
documentation:
Associate a single {class}`Term` with an {class}`Entity`. This creates a Concept entity
if needed and links both the Entity and the Term to the concept, in keeping with the MDB
spec. It supports the Term key in MDF.
\:param Entity ent: {class}`Entity` object to annotate
\:param Term term: {class}`Term` object to describe the Entity

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.add_terms
documentation:
Add a list of {class}`Term` and/or strings to a {class}`Property` with a value domain of `value_set`

```
param Property prop

 to modify

param list terms

A list of  instances and/or str
```

{class}`Term` instances are created for strings;
`Term.value` and `Term.handle` is set to the string.

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.rm_node
documentation:
Remove a {class}`Node` from the Model instance.

```
param Node node

Node to be removed
```

Note: A node can't be removed if it is participating in an edge (i.e.,
if the node is some edge's src or dst attribute)

*Clarify what happens in the Model object, in the database when versioning
is off, in the database when versioning is on*

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.rm_edge
documentation:
Remove an {class}`Edge` instance from the Model instance.

```
param Edge edge

Edge to be removed
```

*Clarify what happens in the Model object, in the database when versioning
is off, in the database when versioning is on*

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.rm_prop
documentation:
Remove a {class}`Property` instance from the Model instance.

```
param Property prop

Property to be removed
```

*Clarify what happens in the Model object, in the database when versioning
is off, in the database when versioning is on*

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.rm_term
documentation: Not implemented.

methods.name: bento_meta.model.Model.assign_edge_end
documentation:
Move the src or dst of an {class}`Edge` to a different {class}`Node`.

```
param Edge edge

Edge to manipulate

param str end

Edge end to change (src|dst)

param Node node

Node to be connected
```

Note: Both `node` and `edge` must be present in the Model instance
(via {meth}`add_node` and {meth}`add_edge`)

Ask whether an entity is present in the Model instance.

modules.name: bento_meta-0.2.10.data.scripts.compare_models
documentation: Command line interface for python script to compare MDB models using fuzzy & nlp matching

documentation:
returns list of fuzzy matching property nodes

:param smdb
:param qstring

documentation: convert results of get_property_synonyms_all() to list of dicts

functions.name: bento_meta-0.2.10.data.scripts.compare_models._join_model_fuzzy_matches
documentation: takes a comp model & row of base_node_prop and returns string with joined fuzzy matches

functions.name: bento_meta-0.2.10.data.scripts.compare_models._join_model_nlp_matches
documentation: takes a comp model & row of base_node_prop and returns string with joined nlp matches

functions.name: bento_meta-0.2.10.data.scripts.compare_models.main
documentation:
Compare base_model's node/property handles to those of comp_model(s)
using fuzzy matching, existing mappings, and NLP.

Args:
    :param str mdb_uri: MDB instance bolt uri
    :param str mdb_user: MDB instance username
    :param str mdb_pass: MDB instance password
    :param str base_model: model name (e.g., GDC)
    :param str comp_models: model names to compare to base model nodes/props
    :param str output_filepath: file path for output CSV
    :param float sim_threshold: threshold for similarty score results
    :param float num_nlp: number of nlp matches to return
        (nlp matches sorted by descending similarity score)

Output:
    .csv file with base node/property handles and any matching node/property handles
